summary: OVERNIGHT RUN. INFRA FIXED: root cause of all writer slowness was a failing Kingston A400 SATA SSD mirrored with the NVMe in pool 'fast' (8-52s write latency) — DETACHED (NVMe had full copy). Then VACUUM completed (16G->13G, freelist 0). Migrated sifter.db+graph.db to ZFS recordsize=16K dataset (fast/siftersearch-db) + synchronous=NORMAL (db.js 2.117.0). Result: writes 29x faster (10k random updates 705ms vs 20734ms). /tank backup verified at /tank/backups/siftersearch/pre-16k-migration-20260618T034508Z. Old files kept at /fast/siftersearch-data/. Entity DB: 4488 entities incl 138 new martyr-roll persons; graph mentions 17925.
overnight_sequence: 1) STEP 2 extract remaining 672 paras (654 footnotes idx 1424-2081 mostly definitions/translations/Quran-citations + few named entities; 13 DB narrative + 5 GPB narrative = high-value). Resolve mentions to EXISTING roster, queue new candidates (don't auto-create from footnotes), mark ALL enriched. 2) STEP 3 deep research every person(~1159)+work(~337): merge dupes (AI adjudication, never keyword), join aliases/kinship, gather cross-corpus. 3) STEP 4 live-build review page from DB (tabs per type, sections per DB chapter by first-introduction, collapsible records) — THE morning deliverable. Plan in scripts/wip/corrections/DB-ENTITY-ROADMAP.md (top).
operational_attention: 1) pool 'fast' now SINGLE NVMe, NO redundancy — re-attach a 2nd NVMe (zpool attach) + retire A400. 2) updater FROZEN (pm2 stop siftersearch-updater) — restart + re-park 4 graph workers when ready. 3) writer path FAST now — normal HTTP write path works, no worker-stop workaround needed.
